untranslatable_reason: Signal requires options-specific parameters (strike prices, premium collection, expiration date logic) that cannot be expressed using equity price/indicator indicators alone. The strangle strategy involves selling two options at different strikes and managing until expiration—this requires options chain data, implied volatility, and time-decay mechanics not available in the standard indicator set. Exit condition depends on absolute strike price levels (upper_strike, lower_strike) which are strategy parameters, not calculable indicators.
translation_notes: This is an options strategy signal (strangle writing) that fundamentally requires options-specific data structures. The entry would need: (1) identification of two strike prices, (2) implied volatility confirmation, and (3) premium received. The exit requires precise strike price thresholds and expiration date tracking. While a ranging regime filter was applied as the signal indicates maximum profit in sideways markets, the core logic cannot be translated to a backtest engine using only equity price/technical indicators.
